entities.description: The state in India where Trichoderma isolates were collected.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of Trichoderma isolates to withstand non-living environmental stressors.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of Trichoderma isolates to withstand living environmental stressors.

entities.name: ANTAGONISTIC ACTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of Trichoderma isolates to inhibit the growth of pathogens.

entities.description: The production of volatile organic compounds by Trichoderma to combat pathogens.

entities.description: The concentration of sodium chloride used to test abiotic stress tolerance.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE

entities.description: Soil infested with pathogens that promotes Fusarium wilt.

entities.description: Controlled environment used for testing isolate efficacy.

entities.name: AUDPC
entities.description: Area Under Disease Progress Curve, used to quantify disease intensity.

entities.description: A chemical fungicide used as a control treatment for Fusarium wilt.

entities.description: Experiments conducted in farmer fields to manage Fusarium wilt.
